steps.stdout: Scheduler starts at t=0 with P1 at the front of the ready queue.
t=0–2: P1 uses the full quantum. It still has 3 ticks, so enqueue it at the tail.
t=2–4: P2 uses the full quantum. It still has 1 tick, so enqueue it at the tail.
t=4–6: P3 uses the full quantum. It still has 5 ticks, so enqueue it at the tail.
t=6–8: P4 uses the full quantum. It still has 2 ticks, so enqueue it at the tail.
t=8–10: P1 uses the full quantum. It still has 1 tick, so enqueue it at the tail.
t=10–11: P2 needs only 1 tick. Mark P2 complete; it does not return to the queue.
t=11–13: P3 uses the full quantum. It still has 3 ticks, so enqueue it at the tail.
t=13–15: P4 finishes exactly at the quantum boundary. Mark P4 complete.
t=15–16: P1 needs only 1 final tick. Mark P1 complete.
t=16–18: P3 uses the full quantum. It has 1 tick remaining, so enqueue it again.
t=18–19: P3 completes. The ready queue is empty, so scheduling ends.
